message.content:
Test file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_10-10-59_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "should add 30% surcharge to base premium for a highly enchanted item (enchantment >= 5)"
Expected: { premium: 145 } for newcomer sword ench 5
Implementation file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_10-10-59_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/claim-office.ts

Add high-enchantment surcharge: when item.enchantment >= 5, add 30% of base premium.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the failing test and implement the minimal code to make it pass.

Now I'll check the math: sword base = 100, ench 5 surcharge = 30 (30%), newcomer surcharge = 10 (10%), processing fee = 5. Total = 100 + 30 + 10 + 5 = 145. ✓

Minimal implementation: add high-enchantment surcharge when `enchantment >= 5`.

content.text:
All tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:

**Implementation**: Added a `highEnchantmentSurcharge` to `premiumForItem` that applies 30% of base premium when `item.enchantment >= 5`. Introduced two constants `HIGH_ENCHANTMENT_SURCHARGE_RATE = 0.3` and `HIGH_ENCHANTMENT_THRESHOLD = 5`.

**Result**: All tests now pass (10 passing, 39 todo).

**Approach**: Mirrored the existing pattern of the cursed surcharge — a simple conditional that adds a percentage of `base` to the per-item premium. Used `(item.enchantment ?? 0)` to handle missing enchantment safely. Verified math: sword 100 + 30% high-ench (30) + 10% newcomer (10) + 5 fee = 145.

Proceeding to Refactor phase.

Files modified:
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_10-10-59_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/claim-office.ts
